description: baseline (e5-base, contrastive, bs=256, lr=1e-5, warmup_ratio=0.33, SHN pool placid-snake-749)
lr=2e-5 — peaked early at 0.6595 then regressed, overshoots short budget
bf16-mixed precision (+0.016, ~43% more steps in budget)
batch_size=512 (+0.007, more in-batch negatives)
batch_size=1024 OOM at 79GB
batch_size=768 also OOMs at 79GB
similarity_scale=30 (-0.004, too sharp)
max_offer_length=192 (-0.007, truncation cost > step gain)
gradient_checkpointing=true bs=1024 — only 950 steps, GC too slow
weight_decay=0.1 — within noise (+0.0001)
n_pos=1 n_neg=3 — hurts, 1 positive per query weakens contrastive signal
n_neg_samples_per_query=4 (+0.004, more SHN per anchor group)
n_neg_samples_per_query=6 — too few queries per batch
lr=5e-6 — undershoots
lr=1.5e-5 — overshoots at bs=512
fresh SHN pool mined from big-stoat-600 — my miner weaker than placid-snake-749 miner
infra: save_top_k=1 to limit disk usage (no metric impact)
warmup_steps=0 — warmup actually matters, no-warmup destabilizes
random_query_pool batching (+0.055, diverse in-batch negatives)
lr=2e-5 + RQP — within noise vs lr=1e-5
fresh SHN pool mined from defiant-mink-296 (+0.005)
output_dim=256 projection head (-0.015, projection hurts)
hard+semi-hard combo (-0.003, rank 0-20 has false positives)
iterative mine from valuable-cod-204 — plateaued
batch_size=640 — within noise, fewer steps cost outweighs larger batch gain
compile=false — within noise, compile marginally helps
num_workers=16 — within noise, data loading not a bottleneck
n_pos_samples_per_query=3 — too few queries per batch
apr14 baseline (e5-base, contrastive, bs=512, lr=1e-5, RQP, SHN pool defiant-mink-296, output_dim=128 projection — pin)
orthogonal init for projection — within noise (-0.003)
2-layer MLP projection (Linear→GELU→Linear) — worse (-0.012), bottleneck wants to be linear
10x LR for projection params (+0.009, fresh head catches up to pretrained encoder)
30x LR for projection params (+0.006, more is more so far)
100x LR for projection params — overshoots, 30x near optimum
50x LR for projection params — within noise of 30x (plateau 30-50x)
similarity_scale=15 (softer) — -0.019, wrong direction; 20 is optimal
encoder lr 2e-5 with projection 30x — within noise (-0.001)
drop ean/article_number/manufacturer_article_number from offer_template — MAJOR regression (-0.056), ID fields are load-bearing (many queries are literal article nums)
natural-sentence offer_template (ID fields preserved) — -0.006 nDCG@5, -0.005 MRR (0.8001 vs 0.8049 champion); current "Field: value" format exposes ID tokens cleanly
training-time random offer field dropout (0.2 per cat/type/brand/desc) — noise, -0.003 nDCG / -0.002 MRR (0.8031 vs 0.8049); regularizers don't help at throughput-bound budget
warmup_ratio=0.1 — -0.003 nDCG, peaked at step 1830 (0.7322) then regressed; shorter warmup destabilizes later training
e5-large (568M) @ bs=256 — -0.015 nDCG, -0.009 MRR; only 1379 steps reached, capacity gain lost to throughput loss at 20-min budget
pre-projection LayerNorm (head group) — noise, -0.002 nDCG / -0.002 MRR (0.8034 vs 0.8049)
auxiliary 768-d contrastive loss on pre-projection pooled (alpha=0.5) — +0.005 nDCG, +0.001 MRR (0.8062); dual-objective preserves full-dim geometry while training 128-d bottleneck
aux_raw_loss_weight=1.0 — -0.007 nDCG vs alpha=0.5, too aggressive; 768-d loss dominates and undertrains 128-d projection
aux_raw_loss_weight=0.3 — -0.004 nDCG vs alpha=0.5, 0.5 is the peak
